sections.title: 1. The Scientific Basis of Time
points: Time is based on the rotation of the earth on its axis from West to East.
The earth completes a full circle of 360° in 24 hours (one day).
The 15° Rule: To find how many degrees the earth moves in one hour, we divide: 360° ÷ 24 = 15° per hour.
The 4-Minute Rule: To find how long it takes to move 1°, we divide 60 minutes by 15°: 60 ÷ 15 = 4 minutes per degree.

sections.title: 2. The Golden Rules of Direction
points: East is Ahead (+): Places in the East see the sun first. Therefore, we add time when moving Eastwards.
West is Behind (-): Places in the West see the sun later. Therefore, we subtract time when moving Westwards.
Reference Point: All time is calculated from the Prime Meridian (0°), also known as Greenwich Mean Time (GMT).

sections.title: 3. The 4-Step Calculation Method
points:
Step 1 (Find Degree Difference):
• If both places are on the same side (e.g., both East), SUBTRACT the degrees.
• If places are on different sides (e.g., East and West), ADD the degrees.
Step 2 (Convert Degrees to Time): Divide the total degrees by 15 to get hours. (If there is a remainder, multiply it by 4 to get minutes).
Step 3 (Find Direction): Determine if the town you are looking for is East or West of the town with the known time.
Step 4 (Final Time): Add or subtract the hours found in Step 2 to the known time.

sections.title: 4. Reverse Calculation: Finding Longitude
points: Sometimes you are given the time and asked to find the location (Longitude).
Step A: Find the time difference between the two places.
Step B: Convert the time into degrees by multiplying the hours by 15°.
Step C: If the unknown place is ahead in time (e.g., PM vs AM), it is East. If it is behind, it is West.

sections.text: Watch Out for AM and PM! If your addition goes past 12:00 noon, the time changes from AM to PM. If you are subtracting from 1:00 PM, remember that 1:00 PM is effectively 13:00 hours in a 24-hour clock!

cards.fact: Equals exactly 1 hour of time difference.
cards.mnemonic: Think of a clock with 24 hours fitting into a 360° circle.

cards.fact: The time it takes for the earth to rotate just 1 degree.
Greenwich Mean Time - the starting point for all world time zones located at 0°.
